content:
Incident Report - INC-4000
Type: Flood
Location: Zone D Date: 2025-06-11 Severity: High
Description:
Heavy rainfall on 2025-06-11 led to waterlogging and flooding in Zone D, disrupting roadways and low-lying areas. The incident was first reported by traffic patrols and multiple citizens via the citys safety alert system. Sewage overflow and clogged drains contributed to rising water levels, affecting both pedestrian and vehicular movement. Local shops and residential basements reported water seepage.
Action Taken:
The municipal drainage team was deployed to pump out water and clear blockages. Barricades were placed to redirect traffic, and warning signs were installed. Emergency alerts were sent to residents advising minimal movement. An urban drainage maintenance project has been prioritized for this zone.

Incident Report - INC-4009
Type: Pothole Location: Zone C Date: 2025-05-28 Severity: High
Description:
On 2025-05-28, a large pothole was reported on a key arterial road in Zone C, causing traffic delays and safety concerns. The pothole, approximately 3 feet wide, appears to have formed due to prolonged water seepage and heavy vehicle movement. Commuters and local businesses flagged the issue repeatedly via the mobile incident reporting app.
Action Taken:
The Roads and Transport team cordoned off the area and began repair work within 12 hours. The damaged section was excavated, re-compacted, and resurfaced using bituminous material. A temporary detour was arranged until the road was reopened. A city-wide pothole survey is now underway.

Incident Report - INC-4013
Type: Electric Fault Location: Zone D Date: 2025-05-10 Severity: Medium
Description:
At approximately 05:59:39 on 2025-05-10, an electrical fault was reported in Zone D, causing partial power outage in nearby buildings. Witnesses noted sparks and a burning smell from a junction box near the sidewalk. Power disruption impacted residential flats and street lighting in the area, and the incident was escalated to city maintenance.
Action Taken:
The electrical maintenance crew responded quickly and isolated the faulty node. Temporary power was restored via an alternate supply loop. The burnt components were replaced, and safety inspections were conducted on adjacent infrastructure. Root cause analysis indicated water seepage. Insulation upgrades have been initiated.

Incident Report - INC-4018
Type: Fire Location: Zone A
Date: 2025-05-04 Severity: Medium
Description:
On 2025-05-04, at around 07:31:02, a fire erupted in a residential/commercial area in Zone A. Initial reports suggest that the cause was likely an electrical short circuit or a flammable substance catching fire. Thick smoke was visible from nearby blocks, and residents immediately reported the incident to local authorities. Multiple housing units were evacuated as a precautionary measure, and emergency services were dispatched promptly.
Action Taken:
The Fire and Rescue Department responded within minutes and deployed multiple fire trucks to the site. The fire was brought under control in approximately two hours. Firefighters ensured no casualties occurred. Authorities temporarily cut power supply to the area, and safety checks were initiated for surrounding buildings. A full inspection and audit of fire safety measures has been scheduled.

The Roads and Transport team cordoned off the area and began repair work within 12 hours. The damaged section was excavated, re-compacted, and resurfaced using bituminous material. A temporary detour was arranged until the road was reopened. A city-wide pothole survey is now underway.

Incident Report - INC-4004
Type: Electric Fault Location: Zone A Date: 2025-05-15 Severity: High
Description:
At approximately 03:02:17 on 2025-05-15, an electrical fault was reported in Zone A, causing partial power outage in nearby buildings. Witnesses noted sparks and a burning smell from a junction box near the sidewalk. Power disruption impacted residential flats and street lighting in the area, and the incident was escalated to city maintenance.
Action Taken:
The electrical maintenance crew responded quickly and isolated the faulty node. Temporary power was restored via an alternate supply loop. The burnt components were replaced, and safety inspections were conducted on adjacent infrastructure. Root cause analysis indicated water seepage. Insulation upgrades have been initiated.
